content.text:
Now I have detailed information about the top 5 richest people in the world as of May 2026:

1. Elon Musk – $802 Billion (Tesla, SpaceX, xAI)
2. Larry Page – $322 Billion (Google/Alphabet)
3. Sergey Brin – $297 Billion (Google/Alphabet)
4. Jeff Bezos – $272 Billion (Amazon)
5. Larry Ellison – $232 Billion (Oracle)

Now I need to create 5 Person objects in the database using the `org.dxos.type.person` schema. 

Creating the Person objects...
I now have the detailed information for the top 5 richest people. Let me create all 5 Person objects in the database:

data.jobTitle: CEO of Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI
data.notes: Richest person in the world as of May 2026 with an estimated net worth of $802 billion. Built an empire spanning electric vehicles, artificial intelligence, robotics, social media, and space exploration. First individual in history to cross the $800 billion mark.

Second richest person in the world as of May 2026 with an estimated net worth of $322 billion. Google cofounder whose fortune jumped massively after Alphabet's stock rallied. One of only three individuals ever to surpass $300 billion in net worth.

Third richest person in the world as of May 2026 with an estimated net worth of $297 billion. Co-founder of Google who benefited heavily from Alphabet's massive AI-driven growth and the increasing value of Google's cloud and advertising businesses.

Fourth richest person in the world as of May 2026 with an estimated net worth of $272 billion. Founder of Amazon who continues to invest heavily in artificial intelligence and aerospace ventures through Blue Origin.

Fifth richest person in the world as of May 2026 with an estimated net worth of $232 billion. Oracle chairman who continues to benefit from the enterprise AI revolution. Oracle's cloud infrastructure business has grown rapidly.

tools.name: query-blueprints
tools.description: Queries available blueprints.

tools.name: enable-blueprints
tools.description: Enables blueprints in the current conversation by their keys. Only blueprints with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled. Always call [query-blueprints] first to discover available blueprint keys.

keys.description: The keys of the blueprints to enable.

tools.name: refresh-blueprints
tools.description: Updates the blueprints saved to the database with the latest version from the registry. Sometimes blueprints in the database can become outdated. Use this function to pull in the latest versions.

tools.name: add-to-context
tools.description:
Adds the object to the chat context.
Use this it for objects that are useful long-term for the conversation.

obj.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: remove-from-context
tools.description:
Removes the object from the chat context.
Use this it for objects that are no longer useful for the conversation.

obj.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

items.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: create-object
tools.description:
Creates a new object and adds it to the current space.
Get the schema from the schema-list tool and ensure that the data matches the corresponding schema.
References are provided in the following format: { "/": "dxn:..." }.
Reference examples: { "/": "dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }, { "/": "dxn:queue:data:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }

tools.name: delete-object
tools.description: Deletes the object.

obj.description:

- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: update-object

obj.description:

- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].

You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query available blueprints and enable them in the current conversation.

<h2><span class="ez-toc-section" id="Top_15_Richest_People_in_the_World_%E2%80%93_May_2026_Rankings"></span><strong>Top 15 Richest People in the World – May 2026 Rankings</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h2>
<p data-start="58" data-end="378">The global billionaire landscape has changed dramatically in 2026, fueled by explosive growth in artificial intelligence, space technology, cloud computing, luxury retail, and semiconductor innovation. The world’s wealthiest individuals are now worth trillions collectively, with fortunes rising faster than ever before.</p>
<p data-start="380" data-end="726">According to the latest Forbes Real-Time Billionaires rankings, the combined wealth of the top 15 richest people in the world exceeds several trillion dollars. Tech entrepreneurs continue to dominate the list, while retail dynasties and luxury business moguls maintain their positions among the global elite.</p>
<p data-start="728" data-end="808">Here’s a detailed look at the top 15 richest people in the world as of May 2026.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1du4npl" data-start="815" data-end="873"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="1_Elon_Musk_%E2%80%93_802_Billion"></span><strong>1. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Elon Musk</span></span> – $802 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="875" data-end="1086">Elon Musk remains the richest person in the world in May 2026. The billionaire entrepreneur has built an empire spanning electric vehicles, artificial intelligence, robotics, social media, and space exploration.</p>
<p data-start="1088" data-end="1392">Much of Musk’s wealth comes from his stakes in Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI. Forbes estimates that SpaceX and xAI alone account for hundreds of billions of dollars in value. Musk also became the first individual in history to cross the $800 billion mark earlier this year.</p>
<p data-start="1394" data-end="1502">His companies continue to benefit from the AI boom and increasing global demand for autonomous technologies.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1j6p2pv" data-start="1509" data-end="1567"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="2_Larry_Page_%E2%80%93_322_Billion"></span><strong>2. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Larry Page</span></span> – $322 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="1569" data-end="1775">Google cofounder Larry Page is now the second-richest person on Earth. Shares of Alphabet surged significantly in 2026 due to advancements in artificial intelligence, cloud computing, and search technology.</p>
<p data-start="1777" data-end="1997">Page’s fortune jumped massively after Alphabet’s stock rallied more than 30% in recent months. He also became one of only three individuals ever to surpass $300 billion in net worth.</p>
<p data-start="1999" data-end="2107">Despite stepping away from daily management years ago, Page still holds a large ownership stake in Alphabet.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1cfqy0d" data-start="2114" data-end="2172"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="3_Sergey_Brin_%E2%80%93_297_Billion"></span><strong>3. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Sergey Brin</span></span> – $297 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="2174" data-end="2384">Sergey Brin, the other cofounder of Google, sits closely behind Larry Page. Brin benefited heavily from Alphabet’s massive AI-driven growth and the increasing value of Google’s cloud and advertising businesses.</p>
<p data-start="2386" data-end="2631">Brin has also become more active in technology development projects within Google, particularly around artificial intelligence initiatives. His fortune continues to rise alongside the company’s market value.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="191arfl" data-start="2638" data-end="2696"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="4_Jeff_Bezos_%E2%80%93_272_Billion"></span><strong>4. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Jeff Bezos</span></span> – $272 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="2698" data-end="2901">Amazon founder Jeff Bezos remains one of the wealthiest individuals in history. Although he stepped down as Amazon CEO years ago, his ownership stake in the company continues to drive his fortune higher.</p>
<p data-start="2903" data-end="3177">Bezos is also investing heavily in artificial intelligence and aerospace ventures through Blue Origin and other startups. His wealth increased significantly in 2026 as Amazon strengthened its dominance in e-commerce and cloud computing.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="15x7y3o" data-start="3184" data-end="3242"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="5_Larry_Ellison_%E2%80%93_232_Billion"></span><strong>5. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Larry Ellison</span></span> – $232 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="3244" data-end="3478">Oracle chairman Larry Ellison continues to benefit from the enterprise AI revolution. Oracle’s cloud infrastructure business has grown rapidly, helping the software giant compete aggressively against Microsoft and Amazon Web Services.</p>
<p data-start="3480" data-end="3623">Ellison’s fortune has expanded steadily due to strong investor confidence in AI-powered cloud services.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="8lswrr" data-start="3630" data-end="3690"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="6_Mark_Zuckerberg_%E2%80%93_210_Billion"></span><strong>6. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Mark Zuckerberg</span></span> – $210 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="3692" data-end="3838">Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg remains one of the world’s richest people thanks to the success of Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and Meta’s AI products.</p>
<p data-start="3840" data-end="4063">Meta’s smart glasses, virtual reality investments, and AI initiatives have significantly boosted investor confidence. Zuckerberg’s wealth climbed sharply during the recent tech rally.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1viumo9" data-start="4070" data-end="4144"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="7_Jensen_Huang_%E2%80%93_Approximately_191_Billion"></span><strong>7. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Jensen Huang</span></span> – Approximately $191 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="4146" data-end="4347">NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang has become one of the biggest winners of the AI era. NVIDIA chips power most of the world’s advanced AI systems, making the company one of the most valuable businesses globally.</p>
<p data-start="4349" data-end="4506">Huang recently surpassed several tech billionaires in wealth rankings as NVIDIA stock continued its explosive growth.</p>
<h3><span class="ez-toc-section" id="8_Michael_Dell_%E2%80%93_Approximately_191_Billion"></span><strong>8. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Michael Dell</span></span> – Approximately $191 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="4589" data-end="4709">Michael Dell’s fortune has surged thanks to Dell Technologies’ growing enterprise infrastructure and AI server business.</p>
<p data-start="4711" data-end="4897">Although Dell briefly traded positions with Jensen Huang in Forbes’ rankings, both billionaires remain among the richest individuals in the world.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1whhlwv" data-start="4904" data-end="4964"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="9_Rob_Walton_%E2%80%93_150_Billion"></span><strong>9. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Rob Walton</span></span> – $150 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="4966" data-end="5172">Rob Walton, heir to the Walmart empire, continues to rank among the richest people alive. Walmart’s strong retail performance and expansion into e-commerce have pushed the Walton family fortune even higher.</p>
<p data-start="5174" data-end="5268">Rob Walton also owns the NFL franchise Denver Broncos.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1q7s2cx" data-start="5275" data-end="5336"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="10_Jim_Walton_%E2%80%93_147_Billion"></span><strong>10. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Jim Walton</span></span> – $147 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="5338" data-end="5515">Jim Walton, the youngest son of Walmart founder Sam Walton, rounds out the global top 10. His wealth comes primarily from Walmart stock and the Walton family’s banking business.</p>
<p data-start="5517" data-end="5630">The Walton family remains one of the richest dynasties in modern history.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="om9m46" data-start="5637" data-end="5705"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="11_Bernard_Arnault_%E2%80%93_Around_142_Billion"></span><strong>11. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Bernard Arnault</span></span> – Around $142 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="5707" data-end="5914">French luxury titan Bernard Arnault narrowly fell outside the global top 10 in May 2026. The LVMH chairman controls some of the world’s biggest luxury brands, including Louis Vuitton, Dior, and Tiffany & Co.</p>
<p data-start="5916" data-end="6037">Although luxury markets slowed slightly, Arnault remains Europe’s richest person.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1sfy0ij" data-start="6044" data-end="6105"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="12_Alice_Walton_%E2%80%93_138_Billion"></span><strong>12. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Alice Walton</span></span> – $138 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="6107" data-end="6241">Alice Walton is currently the richest woman in the world. Her fortune comes from Walmart shares inherited from her father, Sam Walton.</p>
<p data-start="6243" data-end="6382">Unlike her brothers, Alice focused more on philanthropy and the arts rather than retail operations.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="5al4o5" data-start="6389" data-end="6460"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="13_Warren_Buffett_%E2%80%93_Estimated_135_Billion"></span><strong>13. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Warren Buffett</span></span> – Estimated $135 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="6462" data-end="6588">Legendary investor Warren Buffett continues to rank among the richest people globally despite giving away billions to charity.</p>
<p data-start="6590" data-end="6793">Buffett’s investment company, Berkshire Hathaway, owns major stakes in insurance, energy, railroads, and consumer brands. Many investors still consider him one of the greatest financial minds in history.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1cxlx7r" data-start="6800" data-end="6871"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="14_Steve_Ballmer_%E2%80%93_Estimated_130_Billion"></span><strong>14. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Steve Ballmer</span></span> – Estimated $130 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="6873" data-end="6996">Former Microsoft CEO Steve Ballmer has seen his wealth rise due to Microsoft’s continued success in cloud computing and AI.</p>
<p data-start="6998" data-end="7110">Ballmer also owns the NBA’s Los Angeles Clippers and remains one of the richest sports team owners in the world.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1822rif" data-start="7117" data-end="7188"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="15_Mukesh_Ambani_%E2%80%93_Estimated_120_Billion"></span><strong>15. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Mukesh Ambani</span></span> – Estimated $120 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="7190" data-end="7364">Mukesh Ambani remains Asia’s richest businessman and India’s wealthiest person. Reliance Industries dominates sectors including telecom, retail, energy, and digital services.</p>
<p data-start="7366" data-end="7521">Ambani continues to expand Reliance’s footprint in green energy and artificial intelligence, helping him maintain his position among the global ultra-rich.</p>
<h5 data-section-id="37jd4y" data-start="7528" data-end="7566"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="AI_Is_Creating_a_New_Billionaire_Era"></span><strong>AI Is Creating a New Billionaire Era</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h5>
<p data-start="7568" data-end="7764">One major trend defining the 2026 billionaire rankings is artificial intelligence. Companies involved in AI chips, cloud computing, robotics, and machine learning have seen record-breaking growth.</p>
<p data-start="7766" data-end="8052">This AI boom has dramatically increased the fortunes of tech leaders like Elon Musk, Jensen Huang, Larry Page, Sergey Brin, and Mark Zuckerberg. Forbes reported that 2026 has become one of the strongest years ever for billionaire wealth creation.</p>
<h5 data-section-id="1329ug4" data-start="8059" data-end="8075"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="Final_Thoughts"></span><strong>Final Thoughts</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h5>
<p data-start="8077" data-end="8358">The world’s richest people in May 2026 are overwhelmingly connected to technology, artificial intelligence, retail, and digital infrastructure. While traditional industries still create massive fortunes, the modern billionaire economy is increasingly driven by innovation and data.</p>
<p data-start="8360" data-end="8652">Elon Musk continues to dominate the rankings by an enormous margin, while AI-focused companies are producing unprecedented wealth at historic speed. As markets continue evolving, these rankings may change rapidly — but for now, the tech elite remain firmly on top of the global wealth ladder.</p>
<p data-start="8654" data-end="8809">For the latest real-time billionaire updates, Forbes continues to track daily changes in global net worth rankings.</p>

								<a href="https://www.thearabtoday.com/10-ceos-leading-the-biggest-arab-companies-in-the-world/" class="color-line">
									10 CEOs Leading the Biggest Arab Companies in the World								</a>
                        		</h5>
                                <p class="blog-content">

description.description: Optional longer message describing in detail what went wrong

tools.name: query-blueprints
tools.description: Queries available blueprints.

tools.name: enable-blueprints
tools.description: Enables blueprints in the current conversation by their keys. Only blueprints with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled. Always call [query-blueprints] first to discover available blueprint keys.

keys.description: The keys of the blueprints to enable.

tools.name: refresh-blueprints
tools.description: Updates the blueprints saved to the database with the latest version from the registry. Sometimes blueprints in the database can become outdated. Use this function to pull in the latest versions.

tools.name: add-to-context
tools.description:
Adds the object to the chat context.
Use this it for objects that are useful long-term for the conversation.

obj.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: remove-from-context
tools.description:
Removes the object from the chat context.
Use this it for objects that are no longer useful for the conversation.

obj.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

items.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: create-object
tools.description:
Creates a new object and adds it to the current space.
Get the schema from the schema-list tool and ensure that the data matches the corresponding schema.
References are provided in the following format: { "/": "dxn:..." }.
Reference examples: { "/": "dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }, { "/": "dxn:queue:data:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }

tools.name: delete-object
tools.description: Deletes the object.

obj.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: update-object

obj.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: create-relation
tools.description:
Creates a new relation and adds it to the current space.
Get the schema from the schema-list tool and ensure that the data matches the corresponding schema.

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].

You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query available blueprints and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-blueprints] to refresh the list of available blueprints.
  Use [refresh-blueprints] to update blueprint objects stored in the database from the built-in registry when they have drifted.
  Use [enable-blueprints] to enable blueprints by their keys. Always call [query-blueprints] first.
  Only blueprints with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_blueprints>
  -  "Blueprint Manager" -- Query and enable blueprints in the current conversation.
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_blueprints>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the blueprint to use it, only then the tools from that blueprint will appear.
</blueprint>

  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>

Search 5 richest people in the world and create Person objects in the database.

Completion criteria:
- 5 Person objects in the database.
- Web search works.
<input>{}</input>

content.text:
The user wants me to search for the 5 richest people in the world and create Person objects in the database. I need to:

1. Enable the Web Search blueprint to search the web
2. Enable the Database blueprint to create objects
3. Search for the 5 richest people
4. Create Person objects

Let me start by querying blueprints and then enabling the necessary ones.
I'll start by querying available blueprints and then enabling the ones I need (Web Search and Database).

Now let me enable the Web Search and Database blueprints:

locality.description: The locality in which the street address is, and which is in the region.

region.description: The region in which the locality is, and which is in the country.

postOfficeBoxNumber.description: The post office box number for PO Box addresses.

country.description: The country in 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

concurrency.description: Maximum number of concurrent invocations of the trigger. For Feed triggers, this will process Feed items in parallel.
concurrency.title: Concurrency

Let me get more specific information about the top 5. From the search results, I can see:

1. Elon Musk - #1
2. Larry Page - #2
3. Jeff Bezos - likely #3
4. Larry Ellison - likely #4
5. Mark Zuckerberg - likely #5

Let me get more details from one of the pages.

<h2><span class="ez-toc-section" id="Top_15_Richest_People_in_the_World_%E2%80%93_May_2026_Rankings"></span><strong>Top 15 Richest People in the World – May 2026 Rankings</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h2>
<p data-start="58" data-end="378">The global billionaire landscape has changed dramatically in 2026, fueled by explosive growth in artificial intelligence, space technology, cloud computing, luxury retail, and semiconductor innovation. The world’s wealthiest individuals are now worth trillions collectively, with fortunes rising faster than ever before.</p>
<p data-start="380" data-end="726">According to the latest Forbes Real-Time Billionaires rankings, the combined wealth of the top 15 richest people in the world exceeds several trillion dollars. Tech entrepreneurs continue to dominate the list, while retail dynasties and luxury business moguls maintain their positions among the global elite.</p>
<p data-start="728" data-end="808">Here’s a detailed look at the top 15 richest people in the world as of May 2026.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1du4npl" data-start="815" data-end="873"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="1_Elon_Musk_%E2%80%93_802_Billion"></span><strong>1. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Elon Musk</span></span> – $802 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="875" data-end="1086">Elon Musk remains the richest person in the world in May 2026. The billionaire entrepreneur has built an empire spanning electric vehicles, artificial intelligence, robotics, social media, and space exploration.</p>
<p data-start="1088" data-end="1392">Much of Musk’s wealth comes from his stakes in Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI. Forbes estimates that SpaceX and xAI alone account for hundreds of billions of dollars in value. Musk also became the first individual in history to cross the $800 billion mark earlier this year.</p>
<p data-start="1394" data-end="1502">His companies continue to benefit from the AI boom and increasing global demand for autonomous technologies.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1j6p2pv" data-start="1509" data-end="1567"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="2_Larry_Page_%E2%80%93_322_Billion"></span><strong>2. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Larry Page</span></span> – $322 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="1569" data-end="1775">Google cofounder Larry Page is now the second-richest person on Earth. Shares of Alphabet surged significantly in 2026 due to advancements in artificial intelligence, cloud computing, and search technology.</p>
<p data-start="1777" data-end="1997">Page’s fortune jumped massively after Alphabet’s stock rallied more than 30% in recent months. He also became one of only three individuals ever to surpass $300 billion in net worth.</p>
<p data-start="1999" data-end="2107">Despite stepping away from daily management years ago, Page still holds a large ownership stake in Alphabet.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1cfqy0d" data-start="2114" data-end="2172"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="3_Sergey_Brin_%E2%80%93_297_Billion"></span><strong>3. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Sergey Brin</span></span> – $297 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="2174" data-end="2384">Sergey Brin, the other cofounder of Google, sits closely behind Larry Page. Brin benefited heavily from Alphabet’s massive AI-driven growth and the increasing value of Google’s cloud and advertising businesses.</p>
<p data-start="2386" data-end="2631">Brin has also become more active in technology development projects within Google, particularly around artificial intelligence initiatives. His fortune continues to rise alongside the company’s market value.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="191arfl" data-start="2638" data-end="2696"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="4_Jeff_Bezos_%E2%80%93_272_Billion"></span><strong>4. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Jeff Bezos</span></span> – $272 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="2698" data-end="2901">Amazon founder Jeff Bezos remains one of the wealthiest individuals in history. Although he stepped down as Amazon CEO years ago, his ownership stake in the company continues to drive his fortune higher.</p>
<p data-start="2903" data-end="3177">Bezos is also investing heavily in artificial intelligence and aerospace ventures through Blue Origin and other startups. His wealth increased significantly in 2026 as Amazon strengthened its dominance in e-commerce and cloud computing.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="15x7y3o" data-start="3184" data-end="3242"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="5_Larry_Ellison_%E2%80%93_232_Billion"></span><strong>5. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Larry Ellison</span></span> – $232 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="3244" data-end="3478">Oracle chairman Larry Ellison continues to benefit from the enterprise AI revolution. Oracle’s cloud infrastructure business has grown rapidly, helping the software giant compete aggressively against Microsoft and Amazon Web Services.</p>
<p data-start="3480" data-end="3623">Ellison’s fortune has expanded steadily due to strong investor confidence in AI-powered cloud services.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="8lswrr" data-start="3630" data-end="3690"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="6_Mark_Zuckerberg_%E2%80%93_210_Billion"></span><strong>6. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Mark Zuckerberg</span></span> – $210 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="3692" data-end="3838">Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg remains one of the world’s richest people thanks to the success of Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and Meta’s AI products.</p>
<p data-start="3840" data-end="4063">Meta’s smart glasses, virtual reality investments, and AI initiatives have significantly boosted investor confidence. Zuckerberg’s wealth climbed sharply during the recent tech rally.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1viumo9" data-start="4070" data-end="4144"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="7_Jensen_Huang_%E2%80%93_Approximately_191_Billion"></span><strong>7. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Jensen Huang</span></span> – Approximately $191 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="4146" data-end="4347">NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang has become one of the biggest winners of the AI era. NVIDIA chips power most of the world’s advanced AI systems, making the company one of the most valuable businesses globally.</p>
<p data-start="4349" data-end="4506">Huang recently surpassed several tech billionaires in wealth rankings as NVIDIA stock continued its explosive growth.</p>
<h3><span class="ez-toc-section" id="8_Michael_Dell_%E2%80%93_Approximately_191_Billion"></span><strong>8. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Michael Dell</span></span> – Approximately $191 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="4589" data-end="4709">Michael Dell’s fortune has surged thanks to Dell Technologies’ growing enterprise infrastructure and AI server business.</p>
<p data-start="4711" data-end="4897">Although Dell briefly traded positions with Jensen Huang in Forbes’ rankings, both billionaires remain among the richest individuals in the world.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1whhlwv" data-start="4904" data-end="4964"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="9_Rob_Walton_%E2%80%93_150_Billion"></span><strong>9. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Rob Walton</span></span> – $150 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="4966" data-end="5172">Rob Walton, heir to the Walmart empire, continues to rank among the richest people alive. Walmart’s strong retail performance and expansion into e-commerce have pushed the Walton family fortune even higher.</p>
<p data-start="5174" data-end="5268">Rob Walton also owns the NFL franchise Denver Broncos.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1q7s2cx" data-start="5275" data-end="5336"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="10_Jim_Walton_%E2%80%93_147_Billion"></span><strong>10. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Jim Walton</span></span> – $147 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="5338" data-end="5515">Jim Walton, the youngest son of Walmart founder Sam Walton, rounds out the global top 10. His wealth comes primarily from Walmart stock and the Walton family’s banking business.</p>
<p data-start="5517" data-end="5630">The Walton family remains one of the richest dynasties in modern history.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="om9m46" data-start="5637" data-end="5705"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="11_Bernard_Arnault_%E2%80%93_Around_142_Billion"></span><strong>11. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Bernard Arnault</span></span> – Around $142 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="5707" data-end="5914">French luxury titan Bernard Arnault narrowly fell outside the global top 10 in May 2026. The LVMH chairman controls some of the world’s biggest luxury brands, including Louis Vuitton, Dior, and Tiffany & Co.</p>
<p data-start="5916" data-end="6037">Although luxury markets slowed slightly, Arnault remains Europe’s richest person.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1sfy0ij" data-start="6044" data-end="6105"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="12_Alice_Walton_%E2%80%93_138_Billion"></span><strong>12. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Alice Walton</span></span> – $138 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="6107" data-end="6241">Alice Walton is currently the richest woman in the world. Her fortune comes from Walmart shares inherited from her father, Sam Walton.</p>
<p data-start="6243" data-end="6382">Unlike her brothers, Alice focused more on philanthropy and the arts rather than retail operations.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="5al4o5" data-start="6389" data-end="6460"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="13_Warren_Buffett_%E2%80%93_Estimated_135_Billion"></span><strong>13. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Warren Buffett</span></span> – Estimated $135 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="6462" data-end="6588">Legendary investor Warren Buffett continues to rank among the richest people globally despite giving away billions to charity.</p>
<p data-start="6590" data-end="6793">Buffett’s investment company, Berkshire Hathaway, owns major stakes in insurance, energy, railroads, and consumer brands. Many investors still consider him one of the greatest financial minds in history.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1cxlx7r" data-start="6800" data-end="6871"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="14_Steve_Ballmer_%E2%80%93_Estimated_130_Billion"></span><strong>14. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Steve Ballmer</span></span> – Estimated $130 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="6873" data-end="6996">Former Microsoft CEO Steve Ballmer has seen his wealth rise due to Microsoft’s continued success in cloud computing and AI.</p>
<p data-start="6998" data-end="7110">Ballmer also owns the NBA’s Los Angeles Clippers and remains one of the richest sports team owners in the world.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1822rif" data-start="7117" data-end="7188"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="15_Mukesh_Ambani_%E2%80%93_Estimated_120_Billion"></span><strong>15. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Mukesh Ambani</span></span> – Estimated $120 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="7190" data-end="7364">Mukesh Ambani remains Asia’s richest businessman and India’s wealthiest person. Reliance Industries dominates sectors including telecom, retail, energy, and digital services.</p>
<p data-start="7366" data-end="7521">Ambani continues to expand Reliance’s footprint in green energy and artificial intelligence, helping him maintain his position among the global ultra-rich.</p>
<h5 data-section-id="37jd4y" data-start="7528" data-end="7566"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="AI_Is_Creating_a_New_Billionaire_Era"></span><strong>AI Is Creating a New Billionaire Era</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h5>
<p data-start="7568" data-end="7764">One major trend defining the 2026 billionaire rankings is artificial intelligence. Companies involved in AI chips, cloud computing, robotics, and machine learning have seen record-breaking growth.</p>
<p data-start="7766" data-end="8052">This AI boom has dramatically increased the fortunes of tech leaders like Elon Musk, Jensen Huang, Larry Page, Sergey Brin, and Mark Zuckerberg. Forbes reported that 2026 has become one of the strongest years ever for billionaire wealth creation.</p>
<h5 data-section-id="1329ug4" data-start="8059" data-end="8075"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="Final_Thoughts"></span><strong>Final Thoughts</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h5>
<p data-start="8077" data-end="8358">The world’s richest people in May 2026 are overwhelmingly connected to technology, artificial intelligence, retail, and digital infrastructure. While traditional industries still create massive fortunes, the modern billionaire economy is increasingly driven by innovation and data.</p>
<p data-start="8360" data-end="8652">Elon Musk continues to dominate the rankings by an enormous margin, while AI-focused companies are producing unprecedented wealth at historic speed. As markets continue evolving, these rankings may change rapidly — but for now, the tech elite remain firmly on top of the global wealth ladder.</p>
<p data-start="8654" data-end="8809">For the latest real-time billionaire updates, Forbes continues to track daily changes in global net worth rankings.</p>

content.text:
I now have detailed information about the top 5 richest people in the world as of May 2026. Based on the article from The Arab Today, the top 5 are:

1. Elon Musk – $802 Billion (CEO of Tesla, SpaceX, xAI)
2. Larry Page – $322 Billion (Google/Alphabet cofounder)
3. Sergey Brin – $297 Billion (Google/Alphabet cofounder)


4. Jeff Bezos – $272 Billion (Amazon founder)
5. Larry Ellison – $232 Billion (Oracle chairman)

Now I'm creating Person objects for each of them using the `org.dxos.type.person` schema.

I now have the data I need. Based on the May 2026 Forbes rankings, the top 5 richest people are:

1. **Elon Musk** – $802 Billion
2. **Larry Page** – $322 Billion
3. **Sergey Brin** – $297 Billion
4. **Jeff Bezos** – $272 Billion
5. **Larry Ellison** – $232 Billion

Now let me create all 5 Person objects in the database:

data.jobTitle: CEO of Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI
data.notes: Richest person in the world (May 2026). Net worth: ~$802 billion. Built an empire spanning electric vehicles, AI, robotics, social media, and space exploration. First individual to cross the $800 billion mark.

2nd richest person in the world (May 2026). Net worth: ~$322 billion. Alphabet shares surged significantly in 2026 due to advancements in AI, cloud computing, and search technology. One of only three individuals ever to surpass $300 billion.
3rd richest person in the world (May 2026). Net worth: ~$297 billion. Benefited heavily from Alphabet's massive AI-driven growth and the increasing value of Google's cloud and advertising businesses.

4th richest person in the world (May 2026). Net worth: ~$272 billion. Also investing heavily in AI and aerospace ventures through Blue Origin. Wealth increased significantly as Amazon strengthened its dominance in e-commerce and cloud computing.

5th richest person in the world (May 2026). Net worth: ~$232 billion. Continues to benefit from the enterprise AI revolution. Oracle's cloud infrastructure business has grown rapidly, competing against Microsoft and AWS.

4th richest person in the world (May 2026). Net worth: ~$272 billion. Also investing heavily in AI and aerospace ventures through Blue Origin. Wealth increased significantly as Amazon strengthened its dominance in e-commerce and cloud computing.

5th richest person in the world (May 2026). Net worth: ~$232 billion. Continues to benefit from the enterprise AI revolution. Oracle's cloud infrastructure business has grown rapidly, competing against Microsoft and AWS.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].

You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query available blueprints and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-blueprints] to refresh the list of available blueprints.
  Use [refresh-blueprints] to update blueprint objects stored in the database from the built-in registry when they have drifted.
  Use [enable-blueprints] to enable blueprints by their keys. Always call [query-blueprints] first.
  Only blueprints with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_blueprints>
  -  "Blueprint Manager" -- Query and enable blueprints in the current conversation.
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_blueprints>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the blueprint to use it, only then the tools from that blueprint will appear.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  
</blueprint>

Search 5 richest people in the world and create Person objects in the database.

Completion criteria:
- 5 Person objects in the database.
- Web search works.
<input>{}</input>

content.text:
The user wants me to search for the 5 richest people in the world and create Person objects in the database. I need to:

1. Enable the Web Search blueprint to search the web
2. Enable the Database blueprint to create objects
3. Search for the 5 richest people
4. Create Person objects

Let me start by querying blueprints and then enabling the necessary ones.
I'll start by querying available blueprints and then enabling the ones I need (Web Search and Database).

<h2><span class="ez-toc-section" id="Top_15_Richest_People_in_the_World_%E2%80%93_May_2026_Rankings"></span><strong>Top 15 Richest People in the World – May 2026 Rankings</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h2>
<p data-start="58" data-end="378">The global billionaire landscape has changed dramatically in 2026, fueled by explosive growth in artificial intelligence, space technology, cloud computing, luxury retail, and semiconductor innovation. The world’s wealthiest individuals are now worth trillions collectively, with fortunes rising faster than ever before.</p>
<p data-start="380" data-end="726">According to the latest Forbes Real-Time Billionaires rankings, the combined wealth of the top 15 richest people in the world exceeds several trillion dollars. Tech entrepreneurs continue to dominate the list, while retail dynasties and luxury business moguls maintain their positions among the global elite.</p>
<p data-start="728" data-end="808">Here’s a detailed look at the top 15 richest people in the world as of May 2026.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1du4npl" data-start="815" data-end="873"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="1_Elon_Musk_%E2%80%93_802_Billion"></span><strong>1. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Elon Musk</span></span> – $802 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="875" data-end="1086">Elon Musk remains the richest person in the world in May 2026. The billionaire entrepreneur has built an empire spanning electric vehicles, artificial intelligence, robotics, social media, and space exploration.</p>
<p data-start="1088" data-end="1392">Much of Musk’s wealth comes from his stakes in Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI. Forbes estimates that SpaceX and xAI alone account for hundreds of billions of dollars in value. Musk also became the first individual in history to cross the $800 billion mark earlier this year.</p>
<p data-start="1394" data-end="1502">His companies continue to benefit from the AI boom and increasing global demand for autonomous technologies.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1j6p2pv" data-start="1509" data-end="1567"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="2_Larry_Page_%E2%80%93_322_Billion"></span><strong>2. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Larry Page</span></span> – $322 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="1569" data-end="1775">Google cofounder Larry Page is now the second-richest person on Earth. Shares of Alphabet surged significantly in 2026 due to advancements in artificial intelligence, cloud computing, and search technology.</p>
<p data-start="1777" data-end="1997">Page’s fortune jumped massively after Alphabet’s stock rallied more than 30% in recent months. He also became one of only three individuals ever to surpass $300 billion in net worth.</p>
<p data-start="1999" data-end="2107">Despite stepping away from daily management years ago, Page still holds a large ownership stake in Alphabet.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1cfqy0d" data-start="2114" data-end="2172"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="3_Sergey_Brin_%E2%80%93_297_Billion"></span><strong>3. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Sergey Brin</span></span> – $297 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="2174" data-end="2384">Sergey Brin, the other cofounder of Google, sits closely behind Larry Page. Brin benefited heavily from Alphabet’s massive AI-driven growth and the increasing value of Google’s cloud and advertising businesses.</p>
<p data-start="2386" data-end="2631">Brin has also become more active in technology development projects within Google, particularly around artificial intelligence initiatives. His fortune continues to rise alongside the company’s market value.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="191arfl" data-start="2638" data-end="2696"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="4_Jeff_Bezos_%E2%80%93_272_Billion"></span><strong>4. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Jeff Bezos</span></span> – $272 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="2698" data-end="2901">Amazon founder Jeff Bezos remains one of the wealthiest individuals in history. Although he stepped down as Amazon CEO years ago, his ownership stake in the company continues to drive his fortune higher.</p>
<p data-start="2903" data-end="3177">Bezos is also investing heavily in artificial intelligence and aerospace ventures through Blue Origin and other startups. His wealth increased significantly in 2026 as Amazon strengthened its dominance in e-commerce and cloud computing.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="15x7y3o" data-start="3184" data-end="3242"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="5_Larry_Ellison_%E2%80%93_232_Billion"></span><strong>5. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Larry Ellison</span></span> – $232 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="3244" data-end="3478">Oracle chairman Larry Ellison continues to benefit from the enterprise AI revolution. Oracle’s cloud infrastructure business has grown rapidly, helping the software giant compete aggressively against Microsoft and Amazon Web Services.</p>
<p data-start="3480" data-end="3623">Ellison’s fortune has expanded steadily due to strong investor confidence in AI-powered cloud services.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="8lswrr" data-start="3630" data-end="3690"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="6_Mark_Zuckerberg_%E2%80%93_210_Billion"></span><strong>6. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Mark Zuckerberg</span></span> – $210 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="3692" data-end="3838">Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg remains one of the world’s richest people thanks to the success of Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and Meta’s AI products.</p>
<p data-start="3840" data-end="4063">Meta’s smart glasses, virtual reality investments, and AI initiatives have significantly boosted investor confidence. Zuckerberg’s wealth climbed sharply during the recent tech rally.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1viumo9" data-start="4070" data-end="4144"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="7_Jensen_Huang_%E2%80%93_Approximately_191_Billion"></span><strong>7. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Jensen Huang</span></span> – Approximately $191 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="4146" data-end="4347">NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang has become one of the biggest winners of the AI era. NVIDIA chips power most of the world’s advanced AI systems, making the company one of the most valuable businesses globally.</p>
<p data-start="4349" data-end="4506">Huang recently surpassed several tech billionaires in wealth rankings as NVIDIA stock continued its explosive growth.</p>
<h3><span class="ez-toc-section" id="8_Michael_Dell_%E2%80%93_Approximately_191_Billion"></span><strong>8. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Michael Dell</span></span> – Approximately $191 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="4589" data-end="4709">Michael Dell’s fortune has surged thanks to Dell Technologies’ growing enterprise infrastructure and AI server business.</p>
<p data-start="4711" data-end="4897">Although Dell briefly traded positions with Jensen Huang in Forbes’ rankings, both billionaires remain among the richest individuals in the world.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1whhlwv" data-start="4904" data-end="4964"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="9_Rob_Walton_%E2%80%93_150_Billion"></span><strong>9. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Rob Walton</span></span> – $150 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="4966" data-end="5172">Rob Walton, heir to the Walmart empire, continues to rank among the richest people alive. Walmart’s strong retail performance and expansion into e-commerce have pushed the Walton family fortune even higher.</p>
<p data-start="5174" data-end="5268">Rob Walton also owns the NFL franchise Denver Broncos.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1q7s2cx" data-start="5275" data-end="5336"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="10_Jim_Walton_%E2%80%93_147_Billion"></span><strong>10. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Jim Walton</span></span> – $147 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="5338" data-end="5515">Jim Walton, the youngest son of Walmart founder Sam Walton, rounds out the global top 10. His wealth comes primarily from Walmart stock and the Walton family’s banking business.</p>
<p data-start="5517" data-end="5630">The Walton family remains one of the richest dynasties in modern history.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="om9m46" data-start="5637" data-end="5705"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="11_Bernard_Arnault_%E2%80%93_Around_142_Billion"></span><strong>11. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Bernard Arnault</span></span> – Around $142 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="5707" data-end="5914">French luxury titan Bernard Arnault narrowly fell outside the global top 10 in May 2026. The LVMH chairman controls some of the world’s biggest luxury brands, including Louis Vuitton, Dior, and Tiffany & Co.</p>
<p data-start="5916" data-end="6037">Although luxury markets slowed slightly, Arnault remains Europe’s richest person.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1sfy0ij" data-start="6044" data-end="6105"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="12_Alice_Walton_%E2%80%93_138_Billion"></span><strong>12. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Alice Walton</span></span> – $138 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="6107" data-end="6241">Alice Walton is currently the richest woman in the world. Her fortune comes from Walmart shares inherited from her father, Sam Walton.</p>
<p data-start="6243" data-end="6382">Unlike her brothers, Alice focused more on philanthropy and the arts rather than retail operations.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="5al4o5" data-start="6389" data-end="6460"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="13_Warren_Buffett_%E2%80%93_Estimated_135_Billion"></span><strong>13. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Warren Buffett</span></span> – Estimated $135 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="6462" data-end="6588">Legendary investor Warren Buffett continues to rank among the richest people globally despite giving away billions to charity.</p>
<p data-start="6590" data-end="6793">Buffett’s investment company, Berkshire Hathaway, owns major stakes in insurance, energy, railroads, and consumer brands. Many investors still consider him one of the greatest financial minds in history.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1cxlx7r" data-start="6800" data-end="6871"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="14_Steve_Ballmer_%E2%80%93_Estimated_130_Billion"></span><strong>14. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Steve Ballmer</span></span> – Estimated $130 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="6873" data-end="6996">Former Microsoft CEO Steve Ballmer has seen his wealth rise due to Microsoft’s continued success in cloud computing and AI.</p>
<p data-start="6998" data-end="7110">Ballmer also owns the NBA’s Los Angeles Clippers and remains one of the richest sports team owners in the world.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1822rif" data-start="7117" data-end="7188"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="15_Mukesh_Ambani_%E2%80%93_Estimated_120_Billion"></span><strong>15. <span class="hover:entity-accent entity-underline inline cursor-pointer align-baseline"><span class="whitespace-normal">Mukesh Ambani</span></span> – Estimated $120 Billion</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p data-start="7190" data-end="7364">Mukesh Ambani remains Asia’s richest businessman and India’s wealthiest person. Reliance Industries dominates sectors including telecom, retail, energy, and digital services.</p>
<p data-start="7366" data-end="7521">Ambani continues to expand Reliance’s footprint in green energy and artificial intelligence, helping him maintain his position among the global ultra-rich.</p>
<h5 data-section-id="37jd4y" data-start="7528" data-end="7566"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="AI_Is_Creating_a_New_Billionaire_Era"></span><strong>AI Is Creating a New Billionaire Era</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h5>
<p data-start="7568" data-end="7764">One major trend defining the 2026 billionaire rankings is artificial intelligence. Companies involved in AI chips, cloud computing, robotics, and machine learning have seen record-breaking growth.</p>
<p data-start="7766" data-end="8052">This AI boom has dramatically increased the fortunes of tech leaders like Elon Musk, Jensen Huang, Larry Page, Sergey Brin, and Mark Zuckerberg. Forbes reported that 2026 has become one of the strongest years ever for billionaire wealth creation.</p>
<h5 data-section-id="1329ug4" data-start="8059" data-end="8075"><span class="ez-toc-section" id="Final_Thoughts"></span><strong>Final Thoughts</strong><span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h5>
<p data-start="8077" data-end="8358">The world’s richest people in May 2026 are overwhelmingly connected to technology, artificial intelligence, retail, and digital infrastructure. While traditional industries still create massive fortunes, the modern billionaire economy is increasingly driven by innovation and data.</p>
<p data-start="8360" data-end="8652">Elon Musk continues to dominate the rankings by an enormous margin, while AI-focused companies are producing unprecedented wealth at historic speed. As markets continue evolving, these rankings may change rapidly — but for now, the tech elite remain firmly on top of the global wealth ladder.</p>
<p data-start="8654" data-end="8809">For the latest real-time billionaire updates, Forbes continues to track daily changes in global net worth rankings.</p>

content.text:
I now have detailed information about the top 5 richest people in the world as of May 2026. Based on the article from The Arab Today, the top 5 are:

1. Elon Musk – $802 Billion (CEO of Tesla, SpaceX, xAI)
2. Larry Page – $322 Billion (Google/Alphabet cofounder)
3. Sergey Brin – $297 Billion (Google/Alphabet cofounder)


4. Jeff Bezos – $272 Billion (Amazon founder)
5. Larry Ellison – $232 Billion (Oracle chairman)

Now I'm creating Person objects for each of them using the `org.dxos.type.person` schema.

1. **Elon Musk** – $802 Billion
2. **Larry Page** – $322 Billion
3. **Sergey Brin** – $297 Billion
4. **Jeff Bezos** – $272 Billion
5. **Larry Ellison** – $232 Billion

Now let me create all 5 Person objects in the database:

data.jobTitle: CEO of Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI
data.notes: Richest person in the world (May 2026). Net worth: ~$802 billion. Built an empire spanning electric vehicles, AI, robotics, social media, and space exploration. First individual to cross the $800 billion mark.

2nd richest person in the world (May 2026). Net worth: ~$322 billion. Alphabet shares surged significantly in 2026 due to advancements in AI, cloud computing, and search technology. One of only three individuals ever to surpass $300 billion.
3rd richest person in the world (May 2026). Net worth: ~$297 billion. Benefited heavily from Alphabet's massive AI-driven growth and the increasing value of Google's cloud and advertising businesses.

4th richest person in the world (May 2026). Net worth: ~$272 billion. Also investing heavily in AI and aerospace ventures through Blue Origin. Wealth increased significantly as Amazon strengthened its dominance in e-commerce and cloud computing.

5th richest person in the world (May 2026). Net worth: ~$232 billion. Continues to benefit from the enterprise AI revolution. Oracle's cloud infrastructure business has grown rapidly, competing against Microsoft and AWS.
